slogan: Rooted in Tradition, Committed to Progress.
heroTitle: Where Heritage Echoes Through Mountain Stone
heroDescription: Perched in the heart of Mount Lebanon, Hasbaya El Maten is a village steeped in tradition and natural beauty. Known for its stone-built charm and strong communal values, it continues to evolve while preserving the essence of its past.
yourVoiceDescription: Have a suggestion, complaint, or an idea to improve our community? We're listening.
stayConnectedDescription: Follow us on social media for updates & announcements.

historyPage.historyTitle: In a corner of the beautiful upper matn region,
historyPage.historyDescription: Nestled amidst pine, olive, oak, and terebinth trees, Hasbaya spreads across gentle plains, with scattered houses surrounded by apple and pear orchards, and gardens of roses and basil. Every home is infused with the sweet fragrance of these blossoms, perfuming the air and transforming this humble, small town into a vibrant paradise, where nightingales and thrushes roam freely, singing melodious tunes that welcome visitors with joy. As if the Creator, in perfecting this masterpiece of beauty and splendor, wished to shield it from the harshness of nature, He placed it between two mountains, like two mighty guardians standing watch. With its pleasant climate, pure air, fresh waters, and peaceful life, Hasbaya is a place of enchanting charm, crowned with beauty, wrapped in radiance and serenity, embraced by magnificence, and lost in songs of eternal grace and allure.
historyPage.history3Para2: The word Hasbaya is Syriac and means ceramics workshop. There is no doubt that in ancient times—before the current village community—there existed an older civilization, perhaps Aramean or Phoenician, that manufactured pottery in this very village. This is evidenced by the rock-carved sarcophagi, grape and olive presses, numerous pottery fragments, and coins engraved with the image of Christ the King. However, this area, which was part of the district of Keserwan in ancient history, became depopulated following the Mamluk invasions and its destruction at their hands, leading to the dispersal of its inhabitants in the year 1305.
historyPage.history3Para3: Following the victory of the Qaysi faction alongside Emir Haidar Al-Shihabi over their Yamani rivals in the Battle of Ain Dara, Emir Haidar, in 1712, appointed his allies from the Lam'iyin princes as feudal lords over the Metn region. These princes brought their associates to settle in their estates, including the village of Hasbaya, which was part of the Lam'iyin feudal domain based in the town of Metn.
historyPage.history3Para4: A branch of the Hashem family from Aaqoura and a branch of the Abi Diwan family from Falougha came to Hasbaya, and they became known in Hasbaya as Abi Nassar.
historyPage.history3Author: Written by the late Professor Shaheed El-Khoury Bechara El-Hashem
historyPage.history3Source: from the book "Hasbaya of the Metn and Its Notable Figures"

violationProof: A document proving the date of the violation completion
unauthorizedConstruction: I hereby submit my request to obtain an Unauthorized Construction Completion Certificate
permitRequest: I hereby submit my request to obtain an Permit Request
propertyContents: I hereby submit my request to obtain an Property Contents Certificate
occupancyCertificate: I hereby submit my request to obtain an Occupancy Certificate
easementAndZoning: I hereby submit my request to obtain an Easement and Zoning Receipt
appraisalReport: I hereby submit my request to obtain an Appraisal Report.
clearanceNote1: All fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required and must be filled before submitting the request.
clearanceNote2: When collecting the requested certificate, please present the original documents in paper form.
tenantNote1: Show a copy of the legally registered lease contract
tenantNote2: Written consent of the owner signed by the chosen one
clearanceRequestTitle: I am applying to obtain a municipal clearance certificate
duesRequestTitle: I hereby submit my request to inquire about the due municipal fees
taxpayerPlaceholder: Please enter the name of the concerned person
locationNote: Located in the Hasbaya Municipality area
propertyStatement: A comprehensive property certificate dated no more than three months ago
constructionProof: Any document proving the completion of the construction
divisionMaps: If the property is subdivided, subdivision maps must be provided
paymentReminder: Please make sure to settle the residential or non-residential fees due before collecting the request.
processingTimeDetails: Three working days as per Circular No. 8/99 issued by the Minister of Interior and Municipalities.
processingTimeNote: (Processing time may vary based on the current national crisis conditions.)
contactInfo: For inquiries, you may call 70 045 449 from Monday to Friday between 9:00 AM and 1:00 PM.

points.title: When there are violations in the individual units or the shared areas, the clearance process is suspended until the following two conditions are met:
subPoints: The owner of the violating unit must file a lawsuit before the court to remove the violation in the shared areas, stating that the violation occurred without their consent, and register the lawsuit in the land registry.
The owner of the violating unit must submit a notarized affidavit authorizing the municipality or public authority to carry out the demolition.

points.title: A clearance certificate may be granted for a non-violating unit or a unit whose violations have been resolved.
points.note: Clearance will be withheld for the violating unit if a violation exists in the shared area until the lawsuit and the authorization mentioned above are submitted.

comprehensivePropertyStatement: A comprehensive property statement not older than three months
appraisalCommitteeDescription: The appraisal of the square meter sale price of the property's land is conducted by a special committee formed by a decision of the head of the municipality's executive authority. The committee is composed of a municipal council member as chairman, an engineer, and an employee from the Ministry of Interior and Municipalities.
declarationTitle: I hereby request a permit for
boundarySurvey: Survey map with plot markings signed by a certified surveyor (boundaries must be physically marked on site)
surveyMap: Certified copy of the property survey map
networkDiagram: Survey map with property pegs signed by a sworn surveyor (must include visible boundary markers on site)
codeEntryTitle: Enter the Code and Birth Date
name: Name
motherNamePlaceholder: Please enter the mother's name and maiden name
nationalIdPlaceholder: Please enter the national ID or registry number
residenceNote: Please enter your current residence address, the property owner's name, and their phone number
preDisplacementAddress: Please enter the residence address before displacement
backPrefix: Click here to go back to
faqTitle: Know what you need to submit any request

faqItems.title: Violation Construction Report
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
Document proving date of violation
uses: To settle a violation with the Directorate of Urban Planning
To register the construction officially on land records (housing)

faqItems.title: Occupancy Certificate
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
uses: To transfer ownership of property
To request water or electricity subscription
To confirm actual occupancy

faqItems.title: Vacancy Certificate
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
uses: To request tax exemption from municipality or property tax. (Only the owner or legal representative may request this)

faqItems.title: Municipal Clearance
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
If there is construction: proof of construction
If subdivided: attach subdivision maps
uses: To prove no municipal dues remain
For property sale
To renew industrial/commercial/agricultural licenses

faqItems.title: Complaint / Review / Other
documents: ID card or family civil record
uses: Anyone who has a specific request and does not find it among the requests on the platform can submit it through this form.
Anyone who has a specific complaint can submit it through the Muqabha platform, and thus the municipality can contact him when reviewing his complaint.

faqItems.title: Easement & Zoning Certificate
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
uses: To identify planning impacts (roads, utilities...)
Easement to determine the property specifications (classification as villas, industrial, residential, commercial, agricultural, architectural heritage.)
The citizen requests it to know the percentage of the total area of ​​the property that is allowed to be invested in construction
It is required by the property owner or engineer to prepare the building permit

faqItems.title: Appraisal Report Request
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
uses: To estimate square meter price
To proceed with licensing or ownership transfer

faqItems.title: Contents Report
documents: ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
Construction permit / Occupancy / Sale contract (if applicable)
case: Building permit if the building is licensed without housing
Building permit and residential (occupancy) permit if the building is licensed with housing
A copy of the sales contract if the reason for the request is to conduct a sales transaction
uses: To complete a sale, transfer, building permit, electricity subscription application, water subscription application, industrial/agricultural/commercial license renewal application
For electricity/water/industrial/commercial requests

faqItems.title: Fee Inquiry (for taxpayer)
documents: ID card or family civil record
uses: To check dues on the taxpayer

faqItems.title: Fee Inquiry (for property)
documents: ID card or family civil record
A copy of the sales contract if the reason for the request is to conduct a sales transaction
Property certificate no older than 3 months
uses: To check dues on the property

faqItems.title: Permit Request (Renovation, Fencing, Support...)
documents: Copy of ID or family civil registry extract
Property statement issued within the last three months
Easement and zoning statement
Survey map with property markings signed by a sworn surveyor (boundary markers must be present on the property)
Copy of the sales contract if the request is related to a sales transaction
example: Permit to level or excavate the land
Permit to install a tiled tent
Permit to place a ready-made house (container)
Permit to connect the building to the sewage system

faqItems.title: Sewage Connection Certificate
documents: Ownership proof
ID card or family civil record
Property certificate no older than 3 months
uses: To confirm connection to public sewage system
To process housing application
